title: sideritis algarviensis subsp. lusitanica
intro: Sideritis algarviensis subsp. lusitanica, a rare and endemic plant species, is a subspecies of the Sideritis genus, commonly known as Ironwort. It is a medicinal plant rich in flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its various health benefits. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The Sideritis algarviensis subsp. lusitanica plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties and health benefits.
